description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a coarse, granular texture and a mottled appearance. The surface is predominantly gray with scattered white and darker mineral inclusions, giving it a speckled look. The edges are rough and angular, suggesting it was broken from a larger mass rather than smoothed by water or weather. Some faces display more concentrated clusters of lighter minerals, while others are darker and more uniform. There is a visible marking or label on one side, indicating it may have been cataloged or studied. The stone's overall appearance suggests it is dense and solid, typical of igneous or metamorphic rocks found in urban landscaping or construction.
description: A rough, gray stone with a speckled pattern of white and dark mineral inclusions, featuring angular edges and a coarse texture.
description_view_0: This view shows a broad, flat face with a prominent speckled pattern and a visible label or marking on the lower edge.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the stone's angular top and a side with a handwritten marking, with more visible white inclusions.
description_view_2: The stone appears more rounded from this perspective, with a dense concentration of white and gray speckles across the surface.
description_view_3: This side reveals a darker, more uniform face with fewer visible inclusions and a blockier profile.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or edging along a path
attribute_pattern: Mottled speckling with irregular clusters of white and dark mineral inclusions, no regular pattern or artificial markings except for a catalog label.
attribute_weathering: Rough and unpolished, with sharp edges and minimal signs of natural weathering, indicating recent breakage or limited exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically contrasts with manicured lawns or paved surfaces, but can blend with other natural stones in landscaping; may be partially embedded in soil or surrounded by mulch.
